question: What is a 'White Hat' hacker?
options: A hacker who only steals from rich people
An ethical hacker who finds security holes to help fix them
A hacker who wears a literal white hat
A hacker who only works in the winter

question: Which of these makes a password 'Strong'?
options: Using your pet's name so you don't forget
A long mix of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols
Using '12345678' because it is long
Your birthdate

question: What is 'Brute Force' hacking?
options: Breaking a computer with a hammer
Using a program to guess thousands of password combinations until one works
Hacking into a gym's website
Yelling at the computer until it unlocks

question: Why is it dangerous to use 'Public Wi-Fi' (like at a cafe) for online banking?
options: The connection is too slow
Hackers on the same network can 'sniff' your data as it travels through the air
It costs too much money
The cafe might see your screen

question: What is 'Two-Factor Authentication' (2FA)?
options: Having two different computers
Requiring a second 'key' (like a code on your phone) in addition to your password
Typing your password twice
Answering two security questions

question: A hacker finds your password from a data breach at a small game site and tries it on your Gmail. This is called:
options: Credential Stuffing
Lucky guessing
Spamming
Data mining

question: Which of these is the MOST secure 'Security Question' to use?
options: What city were you born in?
What is your mother's maiden name?
A random phrase that only you know the answer to
What high school did you go to?

question: What is 'Shoulder Surfing'?
options: A virus that affects surfers
Watching someone over their shoulder to see their PIN or password
Hacking into a webcam
Using a computer while at the beach

question: What does a 'Password Manager' do?
options: Deletes old passwords
Stores and encrypts all your unique passwords so you only have to remember one 'Master' key
Tells your friends your passwords
Changes your password every day automatically

question:
If you see a 'New Login Detected' email from an app you use,
 but you didn't log in, what should you do?
options: Ignore it; it's probably a mistake
Click 'This wasn't me' and change your password immediately
Reply to the email and say 'Hey!'
Delete the app

question: What is 'Social Engineering'?
options: Building a social media site
Manipulating people into giving up secret info by pretending to be someone they trust
Being very popular online
Fixing a friend's computer

question: Why shouldn't you post photos of your house or school with 'Location Tags' on?
options: The photo looks better without them
It helps hackers or strangers track exactly where you spend your time
It uses too much battery
It makes the file size too large

question:
A hacker calls you and says they are from 'Amazon' 
and need your 2FA code to cancel an accidental order. Should you give it?
options: Yes, to save money
No; companies will never ask for your 2FA code over the phone
Yes, but only if they sound nice
No, unless they know your name

question: What is 'Doxing'?
options: A type of medical hacking
Searching for and publishing private info about someone online to harass them
Winning a game very quickly
Cleaning your computer's hard drive

question: Which of these is a 'Red Flag' that a website is a fake login page?
options: It has a 'Contact Us' page
The URL has extra letters like 'faceb00k.com' instead of 'facebook.com'
It uses blue and white colors
It has a search bar

question: What is the risk of using 'Auto-fill' for passwords on a shared family computer?
options: It makes logging in too fast
Anyone else using that computer can log into your accounts without knowing your password
It uses too much memory
It deletes your history

question: What is a 'VPN' (Virtual Private Network) primarily used for?
options: Making games run faster
Encrypting your internet connection to keep your data private from hackers on the network
Downloading movies for free
Fixing a broken monitor

question: Why is 'Passphrase' (e.g., 'Correct-Horse-Battery-Staple') often better than a complex 'Password' (e.g., 'Tr0ub4dor&3')?
options: It is shorter
It's easier for humans to remember but much harder for computers to crack due to length
It doesn't use any symbols
It is only one word

question: A hacker 'Sim Swaps' your phone. What is their goal?
options: To steal your physical phone
To trick your phone company into moving your number to their device to bypass 2FA
To give you a better data plan
To listen to your music

question: What is 'Piggybacking' in cybersecurity?
options: Carrying a computer on your back
Following an authorized person into a restricted area or using their active session
Hacking into a farm's database
Using two mice at once

question: If you receive a 'friend request' from someone you are already friends with, what is likely happening?
options: They made a second account for fun
A hacker has 'cloned' their profile to try and scam their friends
They accidentally deleted you
Your computer is glitching

question: What is an 'Evil Twin' attack?
options: A hacker who looks exactly like you
A fake Wi-Fi hotspot designed to look like a real one (e.g., 'Starbucks_Guest_Real')
Two viruses attacking at once
A hacked social media account

question: What does it mean if an account has 'Admin Privileges'?
options: It can only play games
It has full control to change settings, install software, and see all files
It is a guest account
It is for kids only

question:
Why is 'Security through Obscurity' 
(e.g., hiding a password in a text file named 'Not_Passwords') bad?
options: The file is too hard to find
Hackers know all the common hiding spots and use tools to search for them
The computer might delete the file
It takes up too much space

question: What is a 'Dictionary Attack'?
options: Throwing a book at a hacker
Using a list of common words and known passwords to try and break into an account
A virus that changes your language
A very slow hacking method

question: What should you check before typing your password into a website?
options: If the site has cool pictures
If the URL is spelled correctly and has a padlock symbol (though padlocks aren't 100% proof)
If the site is popular
If you have enough battery

question: What is 'Juice Jacking'?
options: Stealing someone's drink
When a hacker uses a public USB charging station to steal data or install malware on your phone
Charging your phone too fast
A battery-saving hack

question: Why is it important to 'Wipe' a phone or game console before selling it?
options: To make it look shiny
To remove all your personal accounts and data so the buyer can't access them
To make it weigh less
To speed up the internet

question: What is a 'Session Hijack'?
options: When a hacker takes over your active log-in 'session' after you've already typed your password
When a hacker interrupts your game
When a computer shuts down randomly
When someone steals your keyboard

question: How can you tell if your webcam has been hacked?
options: The screen gets blurry
The indicator light might turn on when you aren't using it (though some hackers can bypass this)
The computer gets hot
The volume goes up
